title: SBA Artificial Intelligence Utilization Act of 2026
action: Placed on the Union Calendar, Calendar No. 594.
summary: The SBA Artificial Intelligence Utilization Act of 2026 is a policy document focused on the regulations and guidelines for the use of Artificial Intelligence within the Small Business Administration. Its purpose is likely to establish a framework for the effective and responsible adoption of AI in SBA operations.

title: Secure and Accountable Military AI Act of 2026
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Armed Services.
summary: The Secure and Accountable Military AI Act of 2026 aims to protect public security by implementing strict rules for artificial intelligence in the military. The act likely focuses on ensuring transparency, accountability, and safety when developing and deploying AI systems in military operations.

title: A bill to require the Secretary of Homeland Security to conduct annual assessments on threats to the United States posed by the use of generative artificial intelligence for terrorism, and for other purposes.
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs.
summary: This policy aims to mandate the Department of Homeland Security to conduct annual assessments of potential threats from using generative AI in terrorism. The goal of these assessments is to identify and counter potential risks posed by AI technology.

title: Artificial Intelligence Environmental Impacts Act of 2026
action: Referred to the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology, and in addition to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The Artificial Intelligence Environmental Impacts Act of 2026 aims to mitigate the environmental effects caused by artificial intelligence systems. The law likely establishes regulations and guidelines for AI developers to minimize the environmental harm associated with their technology.

title: Protecting Consumers from Deceptive AI Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The Protecting Consumers from Deceptive AI Act aims to safeguard consumers from misleading and deceptive artificial intelligence. This act will establish regulations to ensure AI is used transparently and honestly, preventing false or misleading information from being spread through AI.

title: To promote United States leadership in technical standards by directing the National Institute of Standards and Technology and the Department of State to take certain actions to encourage and enable United States participation in developing standards and specifications for artificial intelligence and other critical and emerging technologies, and for other purposes.
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: This document aims to strengthen US leadership in technical standards, specifically in artificial intelligence and emerging technologies. It directs the National Institute of Standards and Technology and the Department of State to promote US involvement in developing these standards.

title: To establish the Department of Advanced Technology and Artificial Intelligence, and for other purposes.
action: Referred to the House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform.
summary: This policy aims to create a Department of Advanced Technology and Artificial Intelligence to handle and develop technology. The department's goals and responsibilities are likely to be outlined in further policy details.

title: Protecting Consumers from Deceptive AI Act
action: Referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, and in addition to the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The Protecting Consumers from Deceptive AI Act aims to safeguard consumers from misleading or dishonest AI practices. This act likely establishes regulations and penalties to hold individuals and companies accountable for using AI in deceptive ways.

title: Heat Emergency Assessment and Tracking using AI Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.
summary: This policy document outlines a proposed Act for using artificial intelligence (AI) to assess and track heat emergencies. The Act aims to provide a framework for utilizing AI in responding to and mitigating the effects of heat emergencies.

title: To provide for a right of action against Federal employees for violations of First Amendment rights relating to the use or development of artificial intelligence.
action: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.
summary: This policy aims to hold Federal employees accountable for violating First Amendment rights regarding artificial intelligence. It seeks to provide a right of action against them when their actions infringe upon these rights.

title: To require the Secretary of Energy to establish a centralized resource for access to data to facilitate biological research through enabling advanced computational methods such as artificial intelligence, and for other purposes.
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: This policy aims to require the Secretary of Energy to create a centralized database for biological research data, making it easier for scientists to access and analyze using advanced computational tools, including AI. The goal is to facilitate scientific research and other purposes.

title: A bill to require the Secretary of Energy to establish a centralized resource for access to data to facilitate biological research through enabling advanced computational methods such as artificial intelligence, and for other purposes.
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources.
summary: This bill aims to create a centralized resource for energy-related biological research data, which can be accessed by researchers using advanced computational methods like AI. It's intended to facilitate and advance biological research for various purposes.

title: Expressing the sense of the House of Representatives with respect to the use of artificial intelligence in the financial services and housing industries.
action: Placed on the House Calendar, Calendar No. 67.
summary: The US House of Representatives expresses its views on the use of artificial intelligence in the financial and housing industries, considering the potential benefits and risks involved. The document aims to provide guidance on the responsible development and implementation of artificial intelligence in these sectors.

title: Small Business Artificial Intelligence Advancement Act
action: Received in the Senate and Read twice and referred to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation.
summary: The Small Business Artificial Intelligence Advancement Act aims to support small businesses in developing and using artificial intelligence. Its specific goals and provisions are not detailed in this title.

title: A bill to require the Office of Financial Research to compel data relating to the financing of artificial intelligence development, provide that data to Congress, and issue recommendations to financial regulatory agencies and Congress to mitigate financial stability risk, and for other purposes.
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs.
summary: This bill aims to require the Office of Financial Research to gather data on AI development financing and provide it to Congress, and issue recommendations to mitigate financial stability risks. The goal is to provide Congress with necessary information and guidance on regulating AI financing to prevent potential financial risks.

title: A bill to authorize certain labor market data collection activities and to improve Federal measurement of the workforce impacts of artificial intelligence, and for other purposes.
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions.
summary: This bill aims to collect labor market data and improve the way the US government measures the impact of artificial intelligence on the workforce. It appears to facilitate the collection of data for research and analysis on the intersection of AI and employment.

title: Protect Elections from Deceptive AI Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on House Administration.
summary: The Protect Elections from Deceptive AI Act aims to safeguard elections from potential manipulation by artificial intelligence. The goal is to prevent AI-generated falsehoods and disinformation from interfering with democratic processes.

title: FARM AI Act of 2026
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry.
summary: The FARM AI Act of 2026 is a proposed law that aims to regulate the use of artificial intelligence in the agricultural industry.

title: A bill to require the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency to carry out a study on the environmental impacts of artificial intelligence data centers and associated energy infrastructure, to require the Director of the National Institute of Standards and Technology to convene a consortium on such environmental impacts, and to require the Administrator to develop a reporting system for the reporting of the environmental impacts of artificial intelligence, and for other purposes.
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Environment and Public Works.
summary: This bill orders the EPA to study the environmental effects of AI data centers, which require a lot of energy. It also requires a consortium to look into these effects and sets up a system for reporting them.

title: Small AI Innovators Empowerment Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The Small AI Innovators Empowerment Act is a policy document aiming to support and empower small AI-related businesses and innovators. The specifics of the act are unavailable.

title: A bill to amend title 10, United States Code, to establish a policy for the Department of Defense on maximizing autonomy and artificial intelligence systems, to establish requirements relating to Department review and verification of autonomous weapon systems, and artificial intelligence capabilities, and for other purposes.
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Armed Services.
summary: This bill aims to establish a policy for the Department of Defense on using artificial intelligence and autonomous systems, while also requiring reviews and verifications for these systems. It seeks to regulate the development and implementation of artificial intelligence capabilities within the Department of Defense.

title: Deterring American AI Model Theft Act of 2026
action: Ordered to be Reported (Amended) by the Yeas and Nays: 43 - 0.
summary: The Deterring American AI Model Theft Act of 2026 is a proposed law that aims to prevent the unauthorized theft of American artificial intelligence models. This act would likely impose penalties and regulations to protect intellectual property related to AI models developed in the United States.

title: NSF AI Education Act of 2025
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The NSF AI Education Act of 2025 is a government policy aimed at implementing and funding AI education programs. The policy likely intends to provide resources and support for educational institutions to develop and teach AI-related courses and programs.

title: Protecting Families from AI Data Center Energy Costs Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.
summary: This bill aims to help protect families from increasing energy costs associated with data centers used by AI technologies. It likely seeks to put in place measures to make the costs more manageable for households.

title: Sectoral AI Governance Act of 2026
action: Referred to the Committee on the Judiciary, and in addition to the Committee on Oversight and Government Reform, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The Sectoral AI Governance Act of 2026 is a proposed law that aims to regulate the use of Artificial Intelligence across different sectors. The specifics of the act are unclear due to the lack of available description.
